a: A request for the age range of a person logged onto the current device.
Information about a person’s age range based on their response to your age range request.
Constants that describe how an adult, parent, or guardian set the age range.
Indicates the person set their own age range using an unspecified method.
Indicates a user’s age range was set using a scrutinized method, like a credit card or government ID.
Indicates the person set their own age range using a government ID.
Indicates a parent, guardian, or Family Organizer in a Family Sharing group set the age range using an unspecified method.
Indicates a parent, guardian, or Family Organizer in a Family Sharing group set the age range.
Indicates a parent, guardian, or Family Organizer in a Family Sharing group set the age range using a government ID.
Indicates a parent, guardian, or Family Organizer in a Family Sharing group set the age range using a payment method, like a credit card.
Indicates the person set their own age range using a payment method, like a credit card.
Indicates the person signed in to iCloud to set their own age range.
An error that occurs when an age range request fails.
Indicates your request contains invalid parameters or configuration.
Indicates the system was unable to share the person’s age range.
An option set to define parental controls enabled and shared as a part of age range declaration.
Defines the regulatory features that your app may need to support.
Indicates the person is required to share their age range with your app.
Indicates that adult users must acknowledge your app’s significant change.
Indicates a parent or guardian is required to acknowledge and consent to a significant app change.
A response indicating whether a person shared their age range or declined to share it.
Indicates the person declined to share their age range with your app.
Contains the person’s shared age range information.
Create age-appropriate experiences in your app by asking people to share their age range.
An action that requests a person’s age range.
Create a significant change flow to inform people about important updates in your app and request age-related permissions.

t: Requesting people’s age range information in your app
a: Ask people to share their age range with your app, and tailor features for adults, teens, and children while preserving privacy.
An action that presents a system sheet for significant app update acknowledgments.
The parental controls that are active on the device.
Information about how the person set their age range.
Returns a response indicating whether the person shared their age range.
Shows the significant update acknowledgment interface.
Indicates that the system limits communication features for the minor.
A list of active parental controls, for debugging purposes.
Creates a parental controls option set with the specified raw value.
A Boolean value that indicates whether an adult, teen, or child is eligible for age-gated features.
The minimum age in the person’s declared age range.
The raw value of the option set.
Requests an age range for the person logged onto iCloud on the device.
Requests an age range for the person signed in to iCloud on the device.
A set of regulatory features that are required for the person.
The singleton app instance for accessing age range services.
Displays a system-provided interface for people to acknowledge a significant app update.
Indicates a notification obligation for significant app updates.
The maximum age in the person’s declared age range.
